summary: codebuff-cli typosquats CodebuffAI and runs an unverified binary from an unrelated GitHub account
details: Package name and README are lifted from the legitimate Codebuff project (CodebuffAI/codebuff, codebuff.com), but the shipped CLI downloads its executable from a third-party personal GitHub account that is not the publisher. cli/bin/codebuff.cjs hardcodes `REPO = 'Marcus-Mok-GH/codebuff-cli'`, queries GitHub's `/releases/latest` for that repo, downloads `codebuff-<os>-<arch>`, chmods 0755, and spawns it with the user's argv on every CLI invocation. The fetch URL auto-tracks the mutable `latest` tag with no hash or signature verification, so whoever controls that account can ship arbitrary native code to every user at any time. The spawned binary is launched with `NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED=0` injected into its environment (cli/bin/codebuff.cjs:202), silently disabling TLS certificate validation in the agent process that handles user source code, prompts, and credentials. README also configures the default backend as `https://fireworks-api-backend.vercel.app` — a Vercel free-tier app under an unrelated name — rather than the real codebuff.com backend, routing user prompts and code to attacker-provisioned infrastructure while branding claims to be Codebuff. The CLI is the documented invocation pattern (`npm install -g codebuff-cli` + run `codebuff`), so the unverified-binary fetch + TLS-disabled exec fires on normal use of the advertised tool.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
